title: Makefile: add target `podman-remote-static`
body:
This allows to build a static `podman-remote` binary for the host architecture.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Cockpit tests failed for commit dc9f2ac40374055fcd64ea4500bae488745afe76. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 4ad87344817d5dfb2afbc5583386b97f022aaf61. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
You need to sign every commit or squash them into a single commit.
Thanks, @rhatdan, I am not used to sign off commits. Sorry!
Cockpit tests failed for commit 77118338272a897c2e29aba5ae821423f638b1fa. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

Nice work. This will fail if there's ever a `podman-remote-static-NONLINUX`, but I'm fine kicking that down the road.

Thank you!

/lgtm

@edsantiago, I thought about setting `GOOS` like this

```Makefile
$(SRCBINDIR)/podman-remote-static-linux_%: GOOS = linux
```

but was not sure if `podman-remote-static` is of any use on non-linux os. Maybe on some bsd?

> is of any use on non-linux os

I seem to remember seeing issues/mail relating to this.  I think the ability to have static podman-remote builds on every `$GOOS` is desirable (if "eventually") by some users.
I agree, if a user wants to only work with podman-remote on Linux then having a static version could be useful.
